message:
fix(fr-store): FUNCTION RESTORE rejects future-version envelope before CRC check (br-frankenredis-r83v self-review)

Self-review of the FUNCTION DUMP envelope work caught a missing
version-check that upstream cluster.c::verifyDumpPayload performs
on every DUMP/RESTORE call: payloads whose 2-byte RDB version
field is HIGHER than the local RDB_VERSION must be rejected with
'ERR DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong' even when the
CRC matches.

Without this gate a future-version FUNCTION DUMP (e.g. one
written by a Redis 7.4-based peer with extended library metadata
fields) would slip past the envelope check, then trip on the body
parser with a confusing wording.

- function_restore: read the 2-byte version field at footer_offset
  and reject with the upstream wording when version >
  FUNCTION_DUMP_RDB_VERSION (currently 11).
- New test function_restore_rejects_payload_with_future_rdb_version
  bumps the version, recomputes the CRC so the version gate is
  what actually fires (not the CRC gate).

fr-store 289 passed; cargo clippy --tests -D warnings clean;
cargo fmt --check clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
